2605.12483.title: Beyond GRPO and On-Policy Distillation: An Empirical Sparse-to-Dense Reward Principle for Language-Model Post-Training
2605.12483.paper_summary: In settings where labeled verifiable training data is the binding constraint, each checked example should be allocated carefully. The standard practice is to use this data directly on the model that will be deployed, for example by running GRPO on the deployment student. We argue that this is often an inefficient allocation because it overlooks a reward-density principle: sparse sequence-level reward should train models where exploration is productive, while dense token-level teacher reward should be used where the aim is to compress behavior into a smaller model. In this view, GRPO-style sparse RL and OPD-style dense teacher supervision are not separate recipes; they are different reward-density regimes. The allocation rule is simple: use scarce labeled training data upstream on the strongest model that can turn it into reward-shaped behavior, then transfer that behavior downstream as dense supervision. We evaluate this rule on verifiable math with Qwen3 and Llama models. At fixed Qwen3-1.7B deployment-student size, an RL-improved 8B teacher distilled through the dense bridge outperforms direct GRPO on the same student, while transfer from the same teacher before RL underperforms. The bridge is important: a forward-KL warmup on teacher rollouts followed by OPD on student rollouts is consistently strongest on MATH before any post-bridge student-side sparse RL, and also gives the best pre-Stage~3 AIME endpoints for the canonical 8B/14B teachers. The bridge also makes later student-side sparse RL effective: GRPO that is weak on a cold student lifts MATH from $75.4\%$ to $78.5\%$ after the bridge and outperforms a matched replay control by $2.8$ points. The operational principal is to avoid using scarce labeled data on the least prepared policy: use sparse reward for teacher-side discovery, dense transfer for student compression, and student-side sparse reward only after the bridge.
2605.12483.paper_summary_zh: 在標記可驗證訓練數據是限制條件的環境中，每個檢查的例子應該被仔細分配。標準做法是將這些數據直接用於將要部署的模型，例如通過在部署學生上運行 GRPO。我們認為這通常是一種低效的分配，因為它忽略了一個獎勵密度原則：稀疏的序列級獎勵應該訓練在探索上有效的模型，而密集的標記級教師獎勵應該用於目的是將行為壓縮成更小模型的情況。在這種觀點下，GRPO 風格的稀疏強化學習和 OPD 風格的密集教師監督並不是獨立的配方；它們是不同的獎勵密度體系。分配規則很簡單：在最強的模型上使用稀缺的標記訓練數據，將其轉化為獎勵形狀的行為，然後將該行為作為密集監督轉移到下游。我們在可驗證的數學上使用 Qwen3 和 Llama 模型評估這一規則。在固定的 Qwen3-1.7B 部署學生大小下，通過密集橋接提煉的 RL 改進 8B 教師在同一學生上超越了直接的 GRPO，而在 RL 之前來自同一教師的轉移表現不佳。橋接是重要的：在教師回放上進行的前向 KL 熱身，隨後在學生回放上進行的 OPD 在 MATH 上始終是最強的，這是在任何橋接後的學生端稀疏 RL 之前，並且也為典型的 8B/14B 教師提供了最佳的前 Stage~3 AIME 端點。橋接還使得後來的學生端稀疏 RL 變得有效：在冷學生上表現不佳的 GRPO 在橋接後將 MATH 從 $75.4\%$ 提升到 $78.5\%$，並且超越了一個匹配的重播控制 $2.8$ 點。操作原則是避免在準備最不足的策略上使用稀缺的標記數據：用稀疏獎勵進行教師端發現，密集轉移用於學生壓縮，而學生端的稀疏獎勵僅在橋接後使用。
2605.12483.author: Yuanda Xu et.al.
2605.12483.authors: Yuanda Xu, Hejian Sang, Zhengze Zhou, Ran He, Zhipeng Wang, Alborz Geramifard
